page_content:
CHEM231 Organic Chemistry I (3 Credits) The chemistry of carbon: aliphatic compounds, aromatic compounds, stereochemistry, arenes, halides, alcohols, esters and spectroscopy. Prerequisite: CHEM131, CHEM135, or CHEM146; and (CHEM132, CHEM136, CHEM147, or CHEM177); and a grade of C- or better in the prerequisites is required of College of Computer, Mathematical, and Natural Sciences majors and recommended for all students. Corequisite: CHEM232. Credit Only Granted for: CHEM104, CHEM231, CHEM233 or CHEM237. Formerly: CHEM233.

CHEM232 Organic Chemistry Laboratory I (1 Credit) Provides experience in developing some basic laboratory techniques, recrystallization, distillation, extraction, chromatography. Prerequisite: CHEM131 and CHEM132; or (CHEM135 and CHEM136); or (CHEM146 and CHEM147). And a grade of C- or better in the prerequisites is required for College of Computer, Mathematical, and Natural Sciences majors and recommended for all students. Corequisite: CHEM231. Credit Only Granted for: CHEM104, CHEM231, CHEM233 or CHEM237. Formerly: CHEM233.
